title: picea menziesii var. crispa
intro: Picea menziesii var. crispa, also known as the Mountain or Rocky Mountain Fir, is a coniferous evergreen tree native to the mountains of western North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat a variety of health issues, such as respiratory problems, fever, and wounds. The health benefits of Picea menziesii var. crispa include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving respiratory function. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other topical applications.
